mnemonics: Execution is like Acting: Execute the script
Method Overriding is like Method Acting: Overturn the original method
Dynamic Method Dispatch is like Method Acting in a Movie: Dispatch the correct method
Using Abstract Classes is like Playing Different Roles: Use abstract classes to play different roles
Final with Inheritance is like Playing a Role for Life: Use final with inheritance to play a role for life
Local Variable Type Inference is like Inferring the Type: Infer the type of local variable
Inheritance is like Inheriting a Trait: Inherit a trait from a parent class
keyPoints: Executed is the main class that contains the main method
Method Overriding is when a subclass provides a different implementation of a method that is already defined in its superclass
Dynamic Method Dispatch is when the correct method is dispatched based on the actual object type at runtime
Using Abstract Classes provides a way to create classes that cannot be instantiated and are intended to be subclassed by other classes
Using final with Inheritance means that a variable can only be assigned a value in the constructor and cannot be changed after that
Local Variable Type Inference allows the compiler to infer the type of a local variable based on its assignment
Inheritance is a mechanism that allows one class to inherit the properties and behavior of another class
